---
title: "Core Concepts in Impact Evaluation in Practice"
description: "Synthesis note on recurring concepts in Impact Evaluation in Practice."
book_id: "impact-evaluation-in-practice"
collection_id: "evaluation-texts"
note_type: "Synthesis"
citation_key: "gertler_martinez_premand_rawlings_vermeersch_2016_impact_evaluation_in_practice"
evidence_ids:
  - "chunk:impact-evaluation-in-practice:what-is-impact-evaluation:c0001"
  - "chunk:impact-evaluation-in-practice:constructing-a-theory-of-change:c0001"
  - "chunk:impact-evaluation-in-practice:causal-inference:c0001"
source_status: "needs_review"
---
## Source

This synthesis draws across the generated notes for *Impact Evaluation in Practice* [@gertler_martinez_premand_rawlings_vermeersch_2016_impact_evaluation_in_practice].

## Central Logic

Impact evaluation estimates causal effects by turning policy questions into testable designs with credible counterfactuals.

## Concepts

- Impact evaluation: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence collection, interpretation, or use in this book.
- Monitoring versus evaluation: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence collection, interpretation, or use in this book.
- Theory of change: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence collection, interpretation, or use in this book.
- Evaluation question: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence collection, interpretation, or use in this book.
- Counterfactual: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence collection, interpretation, or use in this book.
- Power and sampling: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence collection, interpretation, or use in this book.

## Links

- [What Is Impact Evaluation?](section-01-what-is-impact-evaluation.qmd)
- [Constructing a Theory of Change](section-02-constructing-theory-of-change.qmd)
- [Causal Inference and Counterfactuals](section-03-causal-inference-counterfactuals.qmd)

## References
